pattern: Maintain a running 'seen so far' set and a running 'remaining' multiset side by side, updating both in one left-to-right pass instead of recomputing distinct counts per index.
hints: diff[i] only needs two counts: how many distinct values have appeared by index i, and how many distinct values remain after it -- you don't need to recompute either from scratch every time.

Start by tallying every value's total occurrences once, then walk left to right: each time you consume nums[i] on the way, its 'remaining after' count needs to drop, and it needs to join a set of 'seen so far' values.

Track a running set of prefix values and a running multiset of suffix counts (decrement the count for nums[i], removing the key entirely once it hits zero); diff[i] is the prefix set size minus the suffix key count, in one linear pass.

edge_inputs.description: a value reappears later, so it must stay counted in the suffix until its last occurrence is consumed
values at the maximum allowed value

stress.description: random values within the small allowed range, at the max allowed length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
